items.title: Oaktree’s Poli Sees a $200 Billion ‘Distressed Moment’ Coming as Rates Stay High
items.description: Companies with $200 billion in debt face a reckoning as funding costs stay high and debt comes due over the next few years, according to distressed specialist Oaktree Capital Management.

items.title: Liverpool FC Owners Consider Selling Stake to Bhatia-Led Group
items.description: The owners of Liverpool FC are considering selling a stake in the English Premier League football club to a consortium of investors led by Amit Bhatia.

items.title: Viktor Shvets on Whether Geopolitics Matters for Investors
items.description: Tail risks are growing, but they aren’t everything.

items.title: Adobe, Salesforce Downgrades Are Latest Show of AI Fears
items.description: Concerns about the impact artificial intelligence will have on software companies have prompted more Wall Street analysts to recommend selling Adobe Inc. and Salesforce Inc. stock than they have in ye

items.title: SpaceX’s Great Unlocking Begins With $116 Billion Share Release
items.description: SpaceX has set the stage for one of the largest share unlocks in capital markets history, with as much as $116 billion worth of stock becoming eligible for sale for the first time next month.

items.title: Bitcoin Rallies After Bessent Says Clarity Act at ‘1-Yard Line’
items.description: Crypto markets are stirring after months of listless trading on signs that Washington is moving toward a clearer rulebook for digital assets.

items.title: AMC Rolls Out Red Carpet for Debt Investors to Trumpet Rebound
items.description: Dozens of SUVs descended upon AMC’s Lincoln Square theater in New York Monday for an exclusive screening of The Odyssey. But it wasn’t the typical celebrity crowd streaming out of the cars and onto a

items.title: India’s Refiners Come to the Rescue of Russia’s Oil Exporters
items.description: Deliveries of Russian crude oil to India so far this month remain near June’s record

items.title: Pakistan Appoints Banks for its Bond and Sukuk Programs
items.description: Pakistan’s finance ministry has appointed banks for its global bond and sukuk programs, according to a statement.

items.title: US Yields Hit Two-Month High as Oil Sparks Inflation Risk
items.description: US Treasuries fell, pushing 10- and 30-year yields to the highest levels in about two months, as a surge in crude oil prices stoked concern that inflationary pressures will prompt the Federal Reserve

items.title: City of London Sees Steady Hand in Healey as New Chancellor
items.description: Britain’s financial industry welcomed John Healey’s appointment as chancellor of the exchequer, hoping the Treasury’s seventh occupant this decade will deliver stability.

items.title: Kalshi Seeks Approval to List Perpetual Futures Tied to Gold
items.description: Kalshi Inc. is seeking approval to expand precious-metals trading on its platform with a popular type of derivative that never expires for gold, silver and platinum.

items.title: Colombia, Peru Leaders Draw Investors With Policy Signals
items.description: Investors are finding fresh reasons to buy Colombian and Peruvian assets as the countries’ newly elected presidents deliver early signals that their market-friendly agendas will translate into policy

items.title: Oil Tankers Turn Back in Red Sea After Houthi Attack Threat
items.description: Three oil tankers have made U-turns in the southern Red Sea after warnings from Houthi rebels in Yemen that they would target ships heading to or from Saudi ports.

items.title: Kazakh Oil Flows to Black Sea Forced to Stop as Tankers Hit
items.description: Kazakhstan is being forced to stop piping crude to its main export terminal on Russia’s Black Sea coast as a spate of attacks on tankers jeopardizes the landlocked Central Asian country’s exports.

items.title: High-Yielding Emerging-Market Currencies Gain, Stocks Rebound
items.description: Most emerging-market currencies advanced on Tuesday, bolstered by a rebound in risk appetite as investors looked past the latest developments in the Middle East and focused on carry trades instead.

items.title: UK Yields Stall Near Two-Month High in Warning for Burnham
items.description: There’s been no honeymoon in the bond market for the UK’s new Prime Minister Andy Burnham.

items.title: Gold and Silver Rise on Dip-Buying as Traders Weigh Fed Strategy
items.description: Gold and silver rose on dip-buying as traders monitored Middle East tensions for signs that higher energy costs could stoke inflation, putting pressure on the Federal Reserve to tighten policy.

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 21
items.description: Brent oil extended its recent rally as traders weighed threats to maritime traffic that span from the Strait of Hormuz and the Red Sea to a key Kazakh export terminal on Russia’s coast.

items.title: Stocks Climb as Rebound in Chipmakers Gathers Pace: Markets Wrap
items.description: Wall Street staged a comeback as dip buyers emerged, lifting stocks amid rally in chipmakers on bets the artificial-intelligence trade that’s powered the bull market has more room to run.
